display.purpose: Large archive and about headers only.

headline.purpose: Chat empty states, page statements, and compact hero lines.

body.purpose: Transcripts, source snippets, and long-form document reading.

label.purpose: Navigation, metadata, counters, filters, and system states.

shadows.name: cyan-focus-glow
shadows.purpose: Strong focus, active inspection, or selected evidence moments.

shadows.name: overlay-shadow
shadows.purpose: Dialogs, command palette, dropdowns, and sheets only.

motion.name: hero-carousel
motion.purpose: Document carousel changes. Must respect reduced-motion preferences.

motion.name: crt-roll
motion.purpose: Atmospheric viewport overlay. Must never interfere with reading.

components.name: Primary Terminal Button
components.description: Primary source action such as opening chat, browsing the archive, or querying a file.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;border:1px solid #7DD3FC;background:transparent;color:#7DD3FC;padding:12px 24px;border-radius:0;font-family:Geist Mono,ui-monospace,SF Mono,Menlo,monospace;font-size:11px;letter-spacing:.22em;text-transform:uppercase;transition:background-color 180ms ease-out,color 180ms ease-out,border-color 180ms ease-out}.ds-btn-primary:hover{background:#7DD3FC;color:#020506}.ds-btn-primary:focus-visible{outline:2px solid rgba(125,211,252,.45);outline-offset:2px}

components.name: Secondary Terminal Button
components.description: Secondary route or alternate action with hairline border and muted label.

components.name: Composer Field
components.description: Chat query surface with command prompt prefix and action button.
components.css: .ds-composer{display:flex;align-items:flex-start;border:1px solid rgba(220,220,220,.08);background:rgba(5,5,5,.4);border-radius:0;font-family:Geist Mono,ui-monospace,SF Mono,Menlo,monospace;transition:background-color 180ms ease-out,border-color 180ms ease-out}.ds-composer:focus-within{border-color:#7DD3FC;background:rgba(5,5,5,.6)}.ds-composer-prompt{padding:12px 0 0 12px;color:#7DD3FC;font-size:14px}.ds-composer-text{min-height:48px;flex:1;resize:none;border:0;background:transparent;color:#DCDCDC;padding:12px 8px;font:inherit;font-size:14px;line-height:1.6;outline:0}.ds-composer-text::placeholder{color:rgba(107,114,128,.6);font-size:12px;letter-spacing:.16em;text-transform:uppercase}.ds-composer-send{height:36px;margin:8px;padding:0 12px;border:1px solid #7DD3FC;background:transparent;color:#7DD3FC;border-radius:0;font:inherit;font-size:10px;letter-spacing:.18em;text-transform:uppercase}.ds-composer-send:hover{background:#7DD3FC;color:#020506}.ds-composer-send:focus-visible{outline:2px solid rgba(125,211,252,.45);outline-offset:2px}

components.name: Citation Chip
components.description: Inline citation control that opens the exact source page.

components.name: Source Card
components.description: Source rail item with page thumbnail, numeric marker, page number, title, and snippet.

components.name: Archive Filter Input
components.description: Inline filter control for the file index.

components.name: HUD Navigation
components.description: Sticky top-level navigation with compact archive status language.
components.html: CHATUFO // ARCHIVE.001> ARCHIVECHAT
components.css: .ds-hud{height:56px;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:32px;border-bottom:1px solid rgba(220,220,220,.08);background:rgba(2,5,6,.85);color:#6B7280;padding:0 40px;font-family:Geist Mono,ui-monospace,SF Mono,Menlo,monospace;font-size:10px;letter-spacing:.2em;text-transform:uppercase}.ds-hud a,.ds-hud button{border:0;background:transparent;color:inherit;font:inherit;text-decoration:none;transition:color 180ms ease-out}.ds-hud a:hover,.ds-hud button:hover{color:#DCDCDC}.ds-hud .ds-active{color:#7DD3FC}.ds-hud .ds-active span{margin-right:4px}.ds-wordmark{color:#DCDCDC}.ds-wordmark span{color:rgba(107,114,128,.6)}.ds-hud button:focus-visible,.ds-hud a:focus-visible{outline:2px solid rgba(125,211,252,.45);outline-offset:2px}

components.name: Document Page Tile
components.description: Framed page scan tile used in document detail and hero carousel surfaces.

narrative.overview:
ChatUFO should feel like a credible public archive wired into a direct query terminal. The system keeps the existing dark, monochrome, cyan, grid, HUD, and page-preview language, but the craft standard is cleaner than a novelty terminal. The documents are the visual material; the interface is the instrument around them.

The product register is restrained. Users are not here to admire an AI assistant or a themed sci-fi site. They are here to ask, scan, inspect, and verify. Every surface should make the next source action obvious: search the archive, open the document, inspect the cited page, read the transcript, or narrow the scope.

The system explicitly rejects generic SaaS AI chat and conspiracy-site spectacle. No glossy assistant avatars, gradient dashboard polish, alien-fantasy dramatics, panic colors, or claims beyond the corpus. Curiosity is allowed; sensationalism is forbidden.
keyCharacteristics: Source-first: citations, page images, snippets, and metadata stay close to every answer.
Terminal-disciplined: square controls, mono type, uppercase labels, bracketed values, and tight HUD states.
Flat and inspectable: depth comes from hairlines, panels, alpha states, and document imagery, not decorative shadows.
Restrained cyan: cyan marks evidence, action, focus, and selection only.
Atmosphere under control: scanlines, grain, CRT roll, and carousel motion must never fight readability.

rules.name: The One Cyan Rule
rules.body: Evidence Cyan is the only saturated brand color in normal product screens. If a new feature needs more color, it must be semantic data or a true error/warning/success state.

rules.name: The Page Is The Color Rule
rules.body: Document thumbnails, page scans, redactions, and transcripts provide most visual richness. Do not compensate with decorative gradients, loud charts, or illustration.

rules.name: The No Drama Rule
rules.body: Warning Red is reserved for errors and destructive states. Never use red to imply danger, cover-up, alert, disclosure, or conspiracy.

rules.name: The Reading Override Rule
rules.body: Archive labels can be uppercase and spaced out. Answers, transcripts, summaries, and snippets must return to normal-case rhythm when readability matters.

rules.name: The Mono Discipline Rule
rules.body: Use one family. Do not introduce a friendly SaaS sans, a sci-fi display face, or a newspaper serif unless the entire product direction is deliberately reset.

rules.name: The Flat Archive Rule
rules.body: Tables, rails, document cards, empty states, and page grids stay flat at rest. Use a border or a slightly different panel fill before reaching for a shadow.

rules.name: The Overlay Exception Rule
rules.body: Shadows are allowed when a layer floats above the current task, such as command search or a dialog. They are not a general card style.

dos: Do keep the interface credible and source-first: every generated answer should make citations, source pages, and document context easy to inspect.
Do use Evidence Cyan for active navigation, primary action, focus, selected evidence, citation chips, and crop marks.
Do keep document imagery prominent. Page scans, thumbnails, transcripts, snippets, and metadata are the system's most important visual assets.
Do preserve the current terminal/archive identity, but refine noisy areas when they affect comprehension.
Do respect WCAG AA, visible focus states, keyboard navigation, readable long text, and reduced-motion preferences.
Do use square corners and hairline borders for the product shell.
Do make empty states practical: say what is missing and what action fixes it, such as running ingest or adjusting filters.
donts: Don't make ChatUFO feel like a generic SaaS AI chat product. Avoid glossy assistant branding, padded marketing cards, soft gradient dashboards, oversized testimonial-style claims, and vague productivity language.

Don't make it feel like a conspiracy site. Avoid sensationalism, alien-fantasy imagery, fear cues, dramatic red-alert styling, paranormal theatrics, and copy that implies conclusions the archive does not prove.
Don't overplay the terminal aesthetic until it becomes a gimmick. HUD language should support orientation, source inspection, and archive navigation.
Don't add decorative gradients, gradient text, glassmorphism, bokeh, or soft orb backgrounds.
Don't use new colored side stripes on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts. Use full hairline frames, crop marks, labels, or background tint instead.
Don't use heavy shadows on archive surfaces. Save elevation for true overlays.
Don't introduce multiple accent colors for inactive states. Color means evidence, action, focus, selection, or real semantic state.
Don't let scanlines, CRT roll, grain, blinking cursors, or carousel motion interfere with reading or source verification.
